name: Em Dash
description: A long dash equal to the width of the letter M, used to indicate a break in thought or to set off parenthetical information.
usage: Used in place of commas, parentheses, or colons to create emphasis or indicate a parenthetical.

name: En Dash
description: A medium dash equal to the width of the letter N, shorter than an em dash but longer than a hyphen.
usage: Used to indicate ranges (pages 10–20), connections, or as a minus sign in typography.

name: Horizontal Bar
description: A long horizontal line used as a quotation dash in some European languages to introduce dialogue.
usage: Used in some European typographic traditions to mark dialogue instead of quotation marks.

name: Figure Dash
description: A dash the same width as a digit (figure), used in phone numbers and other numeric contexts.
usage: Used in telephone numbers and numeric data where alignment with digit columns is needed.

name: Ellipsis
description: A single character representing three dots used to indicate omitted text or a trailing off in speech.
usage: Used to indicate omitted content in quotations or trailing off in dialogue and informal writing.

name: Hair Space
description: The thinnest space character in Unicode, narrower than a thin space, used for fine typographic adjustments.
usage: Used for subtle spacing adjustments between characters in high-quality typesetting.

name: Thin Space
description: A narrow space character approximately one-fifth the width of an em, used in fine typographic contexts.
usage: Used before colons, semicolons in French typography, and between digits and units in scientific notation.

name: Non-Breaking Space
description: A space character that prevents a line break at its position, keeping adjacent words together.
usage: Used to prevent line breaks between words that should stay together, like units and numbers.

name: Soft Hyphen
description: An invisible formatting hint suggesting where a word may be hyphenated and broken across lines if needed.
usage: Inserted into long words to suggest optional hyphenation break points for text layout engines.

name: Left Single Quotation Mark
description: The typographic opening single quotation mark (smart quote) used in professional typography.
usage: Used as the opening single quote in English and many other languages for professional typesetting.

name: Right Single Quotation Mark
description: The typographic closing single quotation mark (smart quote) also used as an apostrophe in professional typography.
usage: Used as the closing single quote and apostrophe in professional typesetting.

name: Left Double Quotation Mark
description: The typographic opening double quotation mark (smart quote) used in English and many other languages.
usage: Used as the opening double quote in English-language professional typesetting.

name: Right Double Quotation Mark
description: The typographic closing double quotation mark (smart quote) used to close a quoted passage.
usage: Used as the closing double quote in English-language professional typesetting.

name: Euro Sign
description: The currency symbol for the Euro, the official currency of the Eurozone.
usage: Used to denote amounts in Euro currency in financial documents, prices, and UI.

name: Pound Sign
description: The currency symbol for the British Pound Sterling and some other currencies.
usage: Used to denote amounts in GBP and other pound-based currencies.

name: Yen Sign
description: The currency symbol for both the Japanese Yen and the Chinese Yuan Renminbi.
usage: Used to denote amounts in JPY (Japanese Yen) and CNY (Chinese Yuan).

name: Indian Rupee Sign
description: The official currency symbol for the Indian Rupee, adopted in 2010 and combining the Devanagari 'Ra' with a Latin 'R'.
usage: Used to denote amounts in INR (Indian Rupee) in financial contexts.

name: Russian Ruble Sign
description: The official currency symbol for the Russian Ruble, adopted in 2014.
usage: Used to denote amounts in RUB (Russian Ruble) in financial contexts.

name: Bitcoin Sign
description: The official Unicode symbol for Bitcoin, added to Unicode 10.0 in 2017.
usage: Used to denote amounts in BTC (Bitcoin) cryptocurrency.

name: Cent Sign
description: The symbol for one hundredth of various monetary units, primarily the US cent.
usage: Used to denote fractional currency amounts, especially US cents.

name: Won Sign
description: The currency symbol for the South Korean Won and North Korean Won.
usage: Used to denote amounts in KRW (South Korean Won) and KPW (North Korean Won).

name: New Shekel Sign
description: The currency symbol for the Israeli New Shekel.
usage: Used to denote amounts in ILS (Israeli New Shekel) in financial contexts.

name: Rightwards Arrow
description: A simple rightward arrow symbol used to indicate direction, flow, implication, or transitions.
usage: Widely used in UI navigation, breadcrumbs, code comments, diagrams, and mathematical notation.

name: Leftwards Arrow
description: A simple leftward arrow indicating direction, return, or back navigation.
usage: Used in back navigation, revision marks, and logical flow diagrams.

name: Upwards Arrow
description: An upward arrow indicating increase, upload, or upward direction.
usage: Used to indicate increasing values, upload direction, or scroll up in UI contexts.

name: Downwards Arrow
description: A downward arrow indicating decrease, download, or downward direction.
usage: Used to indicate decreasing values, download direction, or scroll down in UI contexts.

name: Left Right Arrow
description: A bidirectional horizontal arrow indicating equivalence, exchange, or two-way relationships.
usage: Used to show bidirectional relations, horizontal resizing, and logical biconditionals.

name: Rightwards Double Arrow
description: A double-lined rightward arrow commonly used in logic and mathematics to denote implication.
usage: Used in logical proofs (A ⇒ B means 'A implies B') and as a stronger directional indicator.

name: Leftwards Double Arrow
description: A double-lined leftward arrow used in logic to denote reverse implication.
usage: Used in logical notation (B ⇐ A means 'B is implied by A').

name: Left Right Double Arrow
description: A double-lined bidirectional arrow denoting logical equivalence or 'if and only if' (iff).
usage: Used in logical proofs to indicate that two statements are logically equivalent.

name: North East Arrow
description: A diagonal arrow pointing to the upper right, indicating upward trend or northeast direction.
usage: Used in charts to show positive trends and in maps for northeast direction.

name: South East Arrow
description: A diagonal arrow pointing to the lower right, indicating downward trend or southeast direction.
usage: Used in charts to show declining trends and in maps for southeast direction.

name: South West Arrow
description: A diagonal arrow pointing to the lower left, indicating southwest direction.
usage: Used in maps and directional diagrams for southwest direction.

name: North West Arrow
description: A diagonal arrow pointing to the upper left, indicating northwest direction.
usage: Used in maps and directional diagrams for northwest direction.

name: Heavy Wide-Headed Rightwards Arrow
description: A bold filled rightward arrow commonly used as a pointer or emphasis in informal contexts and emoji.
usage: Used as a bold directional pointer in lists, social media, and attention-drawing contexts.

name: Plus-Minus Sign
description: The plus-minus symbol indicating a quantity can be both added or subtracted, used for tolerances and approximations.
usage: Used in scientific measurements for uncertainty ranges and mathematical contexts indicating two possible values.

name: Multiplication Sign
description: The typographic multiplication sign, preferred over the letter 'x' in mathematical and scientific notation.
usage: Used in multiplication expressions, dimensions (3×4), and scientific notation.

name: Division Sign
description: The division sign (obelus) representing the division operation in elementary arithmetic notation.
usage: Used in division expressions in elementary mathematics and some calculator notations.

name: Not Equal To
description: The inequality sign indicating two values are not equal to each other.
usage: Used in mathematical expressions and programming documentation to show inequality.

name: Less-Than or Equal To
description: Mathematical symbol indicating a value is less than or equal to another value.
usage: Used in mathematical inequalities, constraints, and algorithm complexity notation.

name: Greater-Than or Equal To
description: Mathematical symbol indicating a value is greater than or equal to another value.
usage: Used in mathematical inequalities, constraints, and comparisons.

name: Almost Equal To
description: The approximation sign indicating two values are approximately (but not exactly) equal.
usage: Used in science and engineering to express approximate equalities and rounded values.

name: Infinity
description: The lemniscate symbol representing the mathematical concept of infinity.
usage: Used in calculus, set theory, limits, and as a general symbol for boundlessness.

name: Square Root
description: The radical sign representing the square root operation in mathematics.
usage: Used in mathematical expressions to denote the square root of a number.

name: Superscript One
description: The digit 1 in superscript form for use in footnotes, exponents, and ordinal notation.
usage: Used as an exponent (x¹), footnote marker, or ordinal suffix in typesetting.

name: Superscript Two
description: The digit 2 in superscript form, commonly used to denote squared values or area units.
usage: Used as a square exponent (x²), in area units (m²), and ordinal notation.

name: Superscript Three
description: The digit 3 in superscript form, commonly used to denote cubed values or volume units.
usage: Used as a cube exponent (x³), in volume units (m³), and ordinal notation.

name: Vulgar Fraction One Quarter
description: Precomposed fraction character representing one quarter (1/4) as a single Unicode glyph.
usage: Used in recipes, measurements, and informal writing where a precomposed fraction is preferred.

name: Vulgar Fraction One Half
description: Precomposed fraction character representing one half (1/2) as a single Unicode glyph.
usage: Used in recipes, measurements, and informal writing for compact fraction display.

name: Vulgar Fraction Three Quarters
description: Precomposed fraction character representing three quarters (3/4) as a single Unicode glyph.
usage: Used in recipes, measurements, and informal writing for compact fraction display.

name: Per Mille Sign
description: Symbol denoting per thousand (parts per thousand), similar to the percent sign but for thousandths.
usage: Used in statistics, finance (interest rates), and chemistry to express parts per thousand.

name: Greek Small Letter Alpha
description: The first letter of the Greek alphabet, widely used in science, mathematics, and statistics.
usage: Used in physics (alpha particles), statistics (significance level), and mathematics.

name: Greek Small Letter Beta
description: The second letter of the Greek alphabet, used in mathematics, physics, and statistics.
usage: Used in finance (beta coefficient), physics (beta particles), and regression analysis.

name: Greek Small Letter Gamma
description: The third letter of the Greek alphabet, used across physics, mathematics, and engineering.
usage: Used for gamma rays, the Euler-Mascheroni constant, and surface tension in physics.

name: Greek Small Letter Delta
description: The fourth Greek letter, commonly used in calculus to denote a small change or partial derivative.
usage: Used in calculus (delta notation), statistics, and physics to indicate change or difference.

name: Greek Small Letter Epsilon
description: The fifth Greek letter, used in mathematics to represent an arbitrarily small positive quantity.
usage: Used in calculus (epsilon-delta proofs), set theory (membership), and machine learning (learning rate).

name: Greek Small Letter Theta
description: The eighth Greek letter, commonly used to represent angles in mathematics and physics.
usage: Used in trigonometry, geometry, and physics to denote angles and angular variables.

name: Greek Small Letter Lambda
description: The eleventh Greek letter, used in physics for wavelength and in computer science for lambda calculus.
usage: Used for wavelength in physics, decay constant in radioactivity, and anonymous functions in programming.

name: Greek Small Letter Mu
description: The twelfth Greek letter, used as the SI prefix for micro (10⁻⁶) and in statistics for the mean.
usage: Used as the micro- prefix (μm, μA), population mean in statistics, and friction coefficient in physics.

name: Greek Small Letter Pi
description: The sixteenth Greek letter representing the mathematical constant ratio of a circle's circumference to diameter (~3.14159).
usage: Used in geometry, trigonometry, and across all fields of mathematics and physics.

name: Greek Small Letter Sigma
description: The eighteenth Greek letter, used in statistics for standard deviation and in physics for various quantities.
usage: Used for standard deviation in statistics, Stefan-Boltzmann constant, and stress in mechanics.

name: Greek Small Letter Phi
description: The twenty-first Greek letter, used to represent the golden ratio and various physical quantities.
usage: Used for the golden ratio (φ ≈ 1.618), magnetic flux in physics, and phase angles.

name: Greek Small Letter Omega
description: The last letter of the Greek alphabet, used in physics for angular frequency and in mathematics for ordinal numbers.
usage: Used for angular frequency in physics, angular velocity, and the first infinite ordinal in mathematics.

name: Greek Capital Letter Omega
description: The uppercase omega, universally recognized as the symbol for the unit of electrical resistance, the Ohm.
usage: Used as the SI unit of electrical resistance (ohms) in electronics and physics.

name: Copyright Sign
description: The copyright symbol indicating a work is protected under copyright law.
usage: Used in copyright notices to declare intellectual property ownership of creative works.

name: Registered Sign
description: The registered trademark symbol indicating a trademark registered with the appropriate government authority.
usage: Used after brand names and logos officially registered as trademarks with a government body.

name: Trade Mark Sign
description: The trademark symbol used to indicate an unregistered trademark, asserting brand ownership.
usage: Used after brand names to assert trademark rights without formal government registration.

name: Section Sign
description: The section sign (silcrow) used in legal documents and academic writing to refer to specific sections.
usage: Used in legal and academic writing to reference specific sections or paragraphs of a document.

name: Pilcrow (Paragraph Sign)
description: The paragraph mark (pilcrow or blind P) used to indicate paragraph breaks in editorial and typographic contexts.
usage: Used in word processors to show hidden paragraph marks, and in legal writing to cite paragraphs.

name: Dagger
description: A typographic cross symbol used as a footnote marker (second reference) or to mark deceased persons in biographies.
usage: Used as a secondary footnote marker after the asterisk, and in genealogy to indicate a deceased date.

name: Double Dagger
description: A double typographic cross (diesis) used as a third footnote marker after the dagger.
usage: Used as the third footnote reference symbol in the traditional sequence: *, †, ‡.

name: Bullet
description: The standard bullet point character used to introduce items in unordered lists.
usage: Used as the primary list item marker in unordered lists in documents and presentations.

name: White Bullet
description: An unfilled (hollow) bullet point used for sub-lists or as a visual alternative to the filled bullet.
usage: Used for nested list items or sub-bullets to visually distinguish hierarchy levels.

name: Triangular Bullet
description: A right-pointing triangular bullet character used as a list marker variant in typesetting.
usage: Used as an alternative bullet style in lists, menus, and navigation indicators.

name: Check Mark
description: A tick or check mark symbol indicating correctness, completion, or affirmation.
usage: Used in checklists, forms, and UI elements to indicate a completed or selected state.

name: Ballot X
description: A cross or X mark symbol indicating incorrectness, rejection, or a false state.
usage: Used in checklists, forms, and UI elements to indicate a failed, rejected, or unchecked state.

name: Warning Sign
description: A triangular warning/caution symbol used to indicate potential hazards or important notices.
usage: Used in UIs, documentation, and safety signage to alert users to warnings or potential issues.

name: Information Source
description: The letter 'i' in a special form indicating information, help, or supplementary details.
usage: Used in UIs as an info icon for tooltips, help text, and additional information buttons.

name: Black Star
description: A solid filled five-pointed star symbol used for ratings, favorites, and decoration.
usage: Used in rating systems (5-star ratings), favorites, awards, and decorative contexts.

name: White Star
description: An outline five-pointed star symbol used alongside black stars for unfilled rating positions.
usage: Used in rating systems to represent unfilled or unselected star positions.

name: Black Heart Suit
description: The heart playing card suit symbol, also used to represent love, likes, and affection.
usage: Used in playing cards, social media likes, and as a love or affection symbol.

name: Black Diamond Suit
description: The diamond playing card suit symbol.
usage: Used in card games and games that use the four traditional playing card suits.

name: Black Club Suit
description: The club (trefoil) playing card suit symbol.
usage: Used in card games and games that use the four traditional playing card suits.

name: Black Spade Suit
description: The spade playing card suit symbol.
usage: Used in card games and games that use the four traditional playing card suits.

name: Black Sun with Rays
description: A sun symbol with radiating rays, used for weather, brightness settings, and decoration.
usage: Used in weather icons, screen brightness controls, and as a general sunny or warm symbol.

name: Cloud
description: A cloud symbol used in weather contexts and also as a metaphor for cloud computing.
usage: Used in weather icons, cloud storage UI, and overcast weather indications.

name: Umbrella with Rain Drops
description: An umbrella with rain drops symbol indicating rainy weather conditions.
usage: Used in weather apps and forecasts to indicate precipitation.

name: Snowflake
description: A decorative snowflake symbol used for winter, cold temperatures, and air conditioning.
usage: Used in weather apps for snow/freezing conditions, and as a winter or cooling indicator.

name: High Voltage Sign
description: A lightning bolt symbol indicating high voltage, electricity, or fast/powerful attributes.
usage: Used for electrical warnings, fast performance indicators, and power/energy contexts.
